title: argemone subalpina
intro: Argemone subalpina, also known as white prickly poppy, is a flowering plant in the poppy family native to the Himalayas. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Argemone subalpina include its potential to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its ability to treat respiratory issues and digestive problems. Herbal preparations of Argemone subalpina can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
